item.title: Exploring causal associations between autoimmune diseases and hearing loss: a mendelian randomization study.
item.description: Brazilian journal of otorhinolaryngology - The causal relationship between Autoimmune Diseases (ADs) and Hearing Loss (HL) remains unclear. This study investigates whether genetic predispositions associated with ADs contribute to HL risk. M...

item.title: IL23R genetic variants associate with Crohn's disease risk and microbiome changes in healthy first-degree relatives.
item.description: Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association - Single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) in the Interleukin-23 receptor (IL23-R) gene are associated with Crohn's disease (CD), suggesting a role in pathogenesis and several biologic agents targeting...

item.title: Gastrointestinal-specific Anxiety in IBD: Associations with Disease Activity, Psychological Symptoms, and Serotonergic Polymorphisms.
item.description: Biopsychosocial science and medicine - Many people living with an inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) experience persistent abdominal symptoms even in the absence of active inflammation. These symptoms are referred to as irritable bowel sy...

item.title: Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor Pathway Is a Potential Biological Mechanism in Inflammatory Bowel Disease-Induced Psoriasis.
item.description: Journal of gastroenterology and hepatology - Psoriasis and inflammatory bowel disease are characterized by relapsing episodes of immune-mediated, chronic inflammation and frequently co-occur. However, the potential causal relationship between...

item.title: Exome sequencing directly implicates 68 genes in inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: medRxiv : the preprint server for health sciences - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a chronic immune-mediated disorder of the gastrointestinal tract whose genetic basis is only partly resolved because most risk variants identified by genome-wide...

item.title: Mitophagy in inflammatory bowel disease: regulatory mechanisms and therapeutic potential.
item.description: Frontiers in cell and developmental biology - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), encompassing Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC), is a chronic inflammatory disorder of the gastrointestinal tract driven by complex interactions amon...
